reforms.description: The Basic Economic and Social Plan FY 1973-77 aims to address pressing social issues such as air pollution and welfare, while maintaining economic growth and stability.
reforms.package_name: Basic Economic and Social Plan FY 1973-77
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan promotes investment in social infrastructure and environmental protection, which can enhance quality of life and long-term economic sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: The Basic Economic and Social Plan was approved by the Government on 13th February 1973.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a significant shift in national priorities towards social welfare and environmental issues, indicating a major reform in Japan's economic policy framework.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Basic Economic and Social Plan, FY 1973-77' approved by the Government on 13th February 1973 is an important expression of this consensus.
